mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or threshold crossing shows no statistical edge (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer durables and real estate demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive returns with >55% accuracy over a 5-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during late-cycle or recession regimes, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month gains in nonfarm payrolls demonstrate sustained labor market expansion and rising household income, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns or produces negative returns in >55% of expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above 0.4% MoM are followed by positive returns in >60% of cases or show no significant predictive alpha.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly expansion in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Combined GDP and retail sales growth fails to generate positive returns in >50% of quarters or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment above 5.0 percent indicates persistent labor market slack and weak consumer spending, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Returns are positive in >55% of months when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent or signal shows no edge in stagnant regimes.
